cards.back: A simple sorting algorithm that works by dividing the list into a sorted and an unsorted region. Each subsequent element from the unsorted region is inserted into the sorted region in the correct position.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort?
cards.back: The average time complexity of Bubble Sort is O(n^2), but it can be improved to O(n) in the best case scenario if the input is already sorted.
A simple sorting algorithm that selects the smallest (or largest) element from the unsorted portion of the list and swaps it with the first element of the unsorted portion.

cards.front: What is the main difference between Insertion Sort and Selection Sort?
cards.back: Insertion Sort operates on a single element at a time, whereas Selection Sort operates on the entire list and selects the smallest (or largest) element to compare and swap.

cards.front: What is the worst-case scenario for Insertion Sort?
cards.back: The worst-case scenario for Insertion Sort is O(n^2), occurring when the input is sorted in reverse order.
